channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK pledges jets, drones and warship for Hormuz defence mission
item.description: The UK announced it will contribute Typhoon jets, mine-hunting drones and HMS Dragon destroyer to a 40-nation mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. Defence Minister John Healey said the mission would begin when conditions allow, with £115m in new funding.

item.title: 💰 US inflation hits 3.8% as Iran war drives energy costs higher
item.description: Consumer prices rose at their fastest rate since May 2023, with energy costs from the Iran war contributing nearly half the increase. The national gas price average hit $4.50/gallon, and airfares jumped 20.7% as jet fuel costs surged.

item.title: 🇺🇬 Ugandas Museveni sworn in for record seventh term after disputed election
item.description: President Yoweri Museveni, 81, began his seventh consecutive term extending his 40-year rule over Uganda. Opposition leader Bobi Wine fled the country claiming the election was marred by ballot-stuffing, while rights groups reported at least 16 killed in post-election violence.

item.title: 🇻🇪 Venezuelas acting president rejects Trumps 51st state comments
item.description: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez told journalists at the International Court of Justice that Venezuela is "not a colony, but a free country" after Trump said he was "seriously considering" making Venezuela the 51st US state. She defended Venezuelas claim to the disputed Essequibo region in the territorial dispute with Guyana.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Hails Test of New Nuclear-Capable ICBM, Calls It World's Most Powerful
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced the test of a new intercontinental ballistic missile, claiming it is the world's most powerful, as Russia continues its nuclear saber-rattling amid the Ukraine war and tensions with the West.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Senate Confirms Kevin Warsh to Federal Reserve Board, Set to Replace Powell as Chair
item.description: The Senate confirmed Trump's pick Kevin Warsh to the Federal Reserve Board, clearing the way for him to take over as Fed chair from Jerome Powell later this week in a significant shift for US monetary policy.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Rejects Iran Peace Plan as 'Garbage' Before Heading to China
item.description: President Trump dismissed an Iran peace proposal before departing for China, where he is expected to seek President Xi Jinping's help in ending the conflict given the lack of progress on diplomatic efforts.

item.title: 🇺🇸💰 US Finalizes $35.5 Billion in Refunds for Illegal Trump Tariffs
item.description: US Customs and Border Protection has received 126,237 applications for tariff reimbursements as companies seek refunds for tariffs that were ruled illegal, a significant economic correction.

item.title: 🇰🇼 Kuwait Foils Iranian Infiltration Attempt on Bubiyan Island
item.description: Kuwait announced it arrested four men accused of being IRGC operatives attempting to infiltrate Bubiyan Island, marking a significant escalation as Gulf states risk being drawn into the Iran conflict.

item.title: 💰 US Iran War Has Cost $29 Billion, Pentagon Tells Congress
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before Congress that the US military campaign against Iran has cost approximately $29 billion, while lawmakers questioned munitions readiness and escalation options.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK PM Starmer Defies Calls to Resign After Heavy Election Losses
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to continue in office despite growing pressure from Labour Party lawmakers urging him to step down following devastating losses in local elections.

item.title: 🏥 WHO Warns Countries to Prepare for More Hantavirus Cases
item.description: The World Health Organization urged nations to prepare for additional hantavirus cases as a cruise ship-linked outbreak spreads, with Spain confirming new cases bringing the total to 11.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky ex-chief of staff Yermak charged in corruption probe
item.description: Andriy Yermak, former chief of staff to President Zelensky and Ukraine's lead negotiator with the US, has been named as a suspect in a major money-laundering investigation tied to luxury development near Kyiv. Six people have been charged in the anti-corruption sweep.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Hamas Weaponized Sexual Violence in October 7 Attacks, Israeli Investigation Finds
item.description: A comprehensive Israeli report documents systematic sexual assaults during the October 7 attacks, detailing harrowing cases. It is the most extensive investigation of its kind to date.

item.title: 🚢☢️ Russian Ship That Sank Near Spain May Have Been Carrying Nuclear Reactors to North Korea
item.description: The cargo vessel Ursa Major, which sank off the Spanish coast, may have been transporting nuclear reactor components destined for North Korea, raising questions about Russia-North Korea military cooperation.

item.title: 🌍 Global Conflict Displacements Hit Record 32.3 Million in 2025
item.description: For the first time, displacements from violence and conflict exceeded those from disasters, with 82.2 million people displaced worldwide. The surge is driven by conflicts in Ukraine, Sudan, and the Middle East.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden Detains Two for Helping Russia Evade Sanctions with High-Tech Shipments
item.description: Swedish Security Service arrested two suspects, a Swedish and Turkish national, for violating EU sanctions by allegedly facilitating advanced technology shipments to Russia that could aid its war in Ukraine.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Minister Vows Continued Support for Ukraine Despite Domestic Political Turmoil
item.description: Europe Minister Stephen Doughty told the Kyiv Independent that Britain remains "resolutely behind Ukraine" even as Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces calls to resign after Labour losses in local elections.

item.title: ⚓ US Navy Reveals Trump Class Battleships Will Be Nuclear Powered
item.description: The Navy annual shipbuilding plan confirms future Trump class BBGNs will use nuclear propulsion, reversing earlier position. Each 35,000-ton warship estimated at $17 billion with hypersonic missiles and railguns.

item.title: 🇱🇧 IDF Uses Robots to Advance Beyond Lebanons Litani River
item.description: Jerusalem Post reports Israeli forces are employing robotic systems to move north of the Litani River in Lebanon, with FPV drones continuing to challenge military operations in the area.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump and Xi Seek to Manage Differences on Iran War During China Summit
item.description: US President Donald Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping are attempting to keep their deep disagreements over the Iran war from overshadowing their summit in Beijing, the first US presidential visit to China in nearly a decade.

item.title: 💰🛢️ US Inflation Surges to 3.8% as Iran War Drives Up Energy Costs
item.description: The key measure of US inflation rose to its highest level since May 2023 as consumers feel the impact of the Iran war on gasoline and energy prices, complicating the economic picture for the Trump administration.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇹🇼 Xi to Press Trump on Taiwan Arms Sales at Beijing Summit
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping is poised to press Donald Trump to slow approval of weapons for Taiwan during their summit, as Beijing calls the self-governing island the "core of China's core interests."

item.title: 🇵🇸🇮🇱 Israel Killing One Palestinian Child Per Week in West Bank, UN Reports
item.description: UN findings show Israeli military and settlers have killed at least one Palestinian child weekly on average in the occupied West Bank amid escalating violence.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚓ Dali Ship Operator Charged Over Baltimore Bridge Collapse That Killed Six
item.description: Six construction workers died when the cargo ship crashed into the Francis Scott Key Bridge. The operator now faces criminal charges.
